title: CaseKhaitanLinTyrrellYuan
summary: Assembles the formal finite-part identity corresponding to Theorem 1.4 from explicit evaluation, recursion, divergence, and renormalization interfaces.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Assembles the formal finite-part identity corresponding to Theorem 1.4 from explicit evaluation, recursion, divergence, and renormalization interfaces.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/Applications/CaseKhaitanLinTyrrellYuan.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Double factorial, with the convention 0!! = 1!! = 1. In explicit terms, its defining expression is | 0 maps to 1 | 1 maps to 1 | n plus 2 maps to n plus 2 times double Factorial n.
for every n : Nat, 0 < double Factorial n.
correspondence.leanType: theorem doubleFactorial_pos : forall n : Nat, 0 < doubleFactorial n

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For n in the natural numbers, double Factorial n is not equal to 0.

The PE specialization of the multiplier in Equation (3.2), at ell = n/2-k. In explicit terms, for n, k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is 2 : Rat to the power n divided by 2 minus k times Nat.factorial n divided by 2 minus 1 : Rat times double Factorial n minus 2 times k minus 1 : Rat divided by Nat.factorial k minus 1 : Rat.

The coefficient in Theorem 1.4. It is defined as the reciprocal of the explicit critical multiplier, avoiding integer exponent coercions and truncated negative powers. In explicit terms, for n, k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is critical Case Multiplier Q n k : Rat : Real ⁻¹.
For n, k in the natural numbers, 0 < critical Case Multiplier Q n k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem criticalCaseMultiplierQ_pos (n k : Nat) : 0 < criticalCaseMultiplierQ n k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The displayed Case coefficient is inverse to the recursion multiplier. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem CaseCoeff_mul_criticalCaseMultiplier (n k : Nat) : CaseCoeff n k * ((criticalCaseMultiplierQ n k : Rat) : Real) = 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Explicit nonnegative-power form of the coefficient in Theorem 1.4. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,n,k\colon \mathbb{N},\quad \operatorname{CaseCoeff} n k = (\mathbb{N}.\operatorname{factorial} (k - 1) \colon \mathbb{R}) / (2 ^ (n / 2 - k) \cdot (\mathbb{N}.\operatorname{factorial} (n / 2 - 1) \colon \mathbb{R}) \cdot (\operatorname{doubleFactorial} (n - 2 \cdot k - 1) \colon \mathbb{R})) = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hMultiplier} \colon ((\operatorname{criticalCaseMultiplierQ} n k \colon \mathbb{Q}) \colon \mathbb{R}) = (2 ^ (n / 2 - k) \cdot (\mathbb{N}.\operatorname{factorial} (n / 2 - 1) \colon \mathbb{R}) \cdot (\operatorname{doubleFactorial} (n - 2 \cdot k - 1) \colon \mathbb{R})) / (\mathbb{N}.\operatorname{factorial} (k - 1) \colon \mathbb{R})
correspondence.leanType: theorem CaseCoeff_eq_closedForm (n k : Nat) : CaseCoeff n k = (Nat.factorial (k - 1) : Real) / ((2 : Real) ^ (n / 2 - k) * (Nat.factorial (n / 2 - 1) : Real) * (doubleFactorial (n - 2 * k - 1) : Real)) := by have hMultiplier : ((criticalCaseMultiplierQ n k : Rat) : Real) = ((2 : Real) ^ (n / 2 - k) * (Nat.factorial (n / 2 - 1) : Real) * (doubleFactorial (n - 2 * k - 1) : Real)) / (Nat.factorial (k - 1) : Real)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Equation (3.2), specialized to a PE metric and the critical iterate, modulo natural divergences. Its mathematical data are: critical recursion mod divergence: for every n k I : Scalar Invariant, 1 < equals k maps to k < equals n divided by 2 maps to I.1.weight equals -2 times k : Int maps to Invariant Evaluation Model.Equal Modulo Divergence Case Laplacian Recursion n k n divided by 2 minus k I Scalar Invariant.qsmul critical Case Multiplier Q n k I.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{critical\_recursion\_mod\_divergence}&\colon \forall n k (I \colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant}), 1 <= k \longrightarrow k <= n / 2 \longrightarrow I.\operatorname{1}.\operatorname{weight} = -2 \cdot (k \colon \mathbb{Z}) \longrightarrow \operatorname{InvariantEvaluationModel}.\operatorname{EqualModuloDivergence} (\operatorname{CaseLaplacianRecursion} n k (n / 2 - k) I) (\operatorname{ScalarInvariant}.\operatorname{qsmul} (\operatorname{criticalCaseMultiplierQ} n k) I)\end{aligned}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The critical ambient invariant i^*(DeltaTilde^(n/2-k) Itilde). In explicit terms, for n, k in the natural numbers, and itilde of type ambient scalar invariant, its defining expression is restrict To Einstein Slice ambient Laplacian Iter n divided by 2 minus k Itilde.
correspondence.leanType: def CaseCriticalInvariant (n k : Nat) (Itilde : AmbientScalarInvariant) : ScalarInvariant

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The abstract integral identity underlying Theorem 1.4, expressed using the renormalized functional in PERenormalizationPackage. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,X\colon \operatorname{PESpace}.\operatorname{}{u, v},\;\operatorname{InvariantEvaluationModel},\;\operatorname{EinsteinAmbientLaplacianFormula},\;\operatorname{CaseEinsteinEvaluationFormula},\;P\colon \operatorname{PERenormalizationPackage} X,\;k\colon \mathbb{N},\;I\colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant},\;Itilde\colon \operatorname{AmbientScalarInvariant},\;hn\colon \operatorname{Even} X.\operatorname{bulkDim},\;hkpos\colon 1 <= k,\;hkrange\colon k <= X.\operatorname{bulkDim} / 2,\;hStraight\colon \operatorname{ScalarStraightenable} I \operatorname{Itilde},\;hWeight\colon I.\operatorname{1}.\operatorname{weight} = -2 \cdot (k \colon \mathbb{Z}),\quad P.\operatorname{RInt} I = \operatorname{CaseCoeff} X.\operatorname{bulkDim} k \cdot P.\operatorname{convergentIntegral} (\operatorname{CaseCriticalInvariant} X.\operatorname{bulkDim} k \operatorname{Itilde})
correspondence.leanType: theorem theorem_1_4_renormalizedIntegral (X : PESpace.{u, v}) [InvariantEvaluationModel] [EinsteinAmbientLaplacianFormula] [CaseEinsteinEvaluationFormula] [P : PERenormalizationPackage X] (k : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) (Itilde : AmbientScalarInvariant) (hn : Even X.bulkDim) (hkpos : 1 <= k) (hkrange : k <= X.bulkDim / 2) (hStraight : ScalarStraightenable I Itilde) (hWeight : I.1.weight = -2 * (k : Int)) : P.RInt I = CaseCoeff X.bulkDim k * P.convergentIntegral (CaseCriticalInvariant X.bulkDim k Itilde)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Theorem 1.4 with the left-hand side written as the Phase B finite-part renormalized curvature integral. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,X\colon \operatorname{PESpace}.\operatorname{}{u, v},\;\operatorname{InvariantEvaluationModel},\;\operatorname{EinsteinAmbientLaplacianFormula},\;\operatorname{CaseEinsteinEvaluationFormula},\;P\colon \operatorname{PERenormalizationPackage} X,\;k\colon \mathbb{N},\;I\colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant},\;Itilde\colon \operatorname{AmbientScalarInvariant},\;hn\colon \operatorname{Even} X.\operatorname{bulkDim},\;hkpos\colon 1 <= k,\;hkrange\colon k <= X.\operatorname{bulkDim} / 2,\;hStraight\colon \operatorname{ScalarStraightenable} I \operatorname{Itilde},\;hWeight\colon I.\operatorname{1}.\operatorname{weight} = -2 \cdot (k \colon \mathbb{Z}),\quad \operatorname{RenormalizedCurvatureIntegral} (P.\operatorname{cutoffData} I) = \operatorname{CaseCoeff} X.\operatorname{bulkDim} k \cdot P.\operatorname{convergentIntegral} (\operatorname{CaseCriticalInvariant} X.\operatorname{bulkDim} k \operatorname{Itilde})
correspondence.leanType: theorem theorem_1_4_formal (X : PESpace.{u, v}) [InvariantEvaluationModel] [EinsteinAmbientLaplacianFormula] [CaseEinsteinEvaluationFormula] [P : PERenormalizationPackage X] (k : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) (Itilde : AmbientScalarInvariant) (hn : Even X.bulkDim) (hkpos : 1 <= k) (hkrange : k <= X.bulkDim / 2) (hStraight : ScalarStraightenable I Itilde) (hWeight : I.1.weight = -2 * (k : Int)) : RenormalizedCurvatureIntegral (P.cutoffData I) = CaseCoeff X.bulkDim k * P.convergentIntegral (CaseCriticalInvariant X.bulkDim k Itilde)

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
